target.title: Add progress draft label ellipsis option
target.body:
# Add optional ellipsis rendering for progress draft labels

## Summary

Adds an opt-in `streaming.progress.ellipsis` setting for progress draft labels:

- `"off"` keeps current behavior and renders the selected label unchanged.
- `"static"` appends `...` to the progress label.
- `"animated"` cycles the label through no suffix, `.`, `..`, and `...`.

Telegram progress drafts also refresh while an animated progress-only draft is active, so the ellipsis moves even when no new tool-progress lines arrive. The refresh timer is cleared when the draft is reset, finalized, superseded, or the dispatch exits.

Example:

```json5
channels: {
  telegram: {
    streaming: {
      mode: "progress",
      progress: {
        label: "auto",
        ellipsis: "animated"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Why

Progress draft labels such as `Working`, `Shelling`, or custom label pools can feel static during long tool calls. This adds a small renderer-level option so users can get classic loading punctuation without baking dots into their configured label list.

Keeping this at render time preserves reusable labels and avoids forcing all users into animated edits.

## Notes

- Default remains `"off"` to preserve current behavior and avoid extra message edits.
- Shared formatter support applies to all channels that use `formatChannelProgressDraftText`.
- True timer-driven animation is currently implemented for Telegram, where progress mode edits a single draft message.
- Other channels will render the configured ellipsis when progress text is re-rendered; they can add timer-driven refreshes later if desired.
- Labels that already end in `.`, `..`, `...`, or `…` are not double-suffixed.

## Testing

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `pnpm run config:schema:check`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The new `streaming.progress.ellipsis: "animated"` Telegram progress draft option should visibly cycle the draft label suffix during a long-running turn and stop once the final answer is delivered.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS with the Telegram channel enabled, Telegram Desktop viewing the bot conversation, and the patched progress draft renderer active.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Configured Telegram progress streaming with `streaming.mode: "progress"`, `progress.label: "auto"`, and `progress.ellipsis: "animated"`; restarted the gateway; sent a Telegram request that triggered a long-running Codex/tool turn; observed the progress draft through final delivery.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted Telegram Desktop GIF: ![Telegram progress ellipsis proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rowbotik/openclaw/proof-progress-label-ellipsis/telegram-progress-ellipsis.gif)

**Observed result after fix:** The Telegram progress draft label visibly cycles through the ellipsis states while the turn is running, then the progress draft is cleared/stopped when the final answer is delivered.

**What was not tested:** Timer-driven animation on non-Telegram channels; those channels still use the shared formatter when they re-render progress text, but this PR only adds active refresh behavior for Telegram.
target.author: rowbotik
